query: ok our docs are a mess. BACKEND.md says we have 101 tables in one place, 106 in another and 114 in the footer. CLAUDE.md references a helper called makeAsyncAction that doesn't exist anywhere in the codebase. can you do a real deep dive and reconcile the docs with the actual app?

i just ran graphify on lex_council/apps and it found 1542 weakly-connected nodes and a god-object candidate. before I touch anything I want to verify this against code reality and figure out which docs are out of sync. lots of moving parts so probably needs multiple agents in parallel

we're cutting v1.7.0 next week. before we ship i want a full audit — does the code match the architecture docs, are there ghost references, dead routes, misnamed views, that kind of thing. the project is ~700 source files so it'll need to be split up
doc sync time. give me a campaign with parallel subagents covering backend reality, frontend reality, integration, rule compliance, then synthesize and update the planet hub docs

I inherited this codebase from a contractor and the docs are super stale. Three different counts of triggers in the same file, references to tables that don't exist anymore, and a list of edge functions where some are deployed and some aren't. Help me get this back to a single source of truth.
spin up a multi-wave audit on the current dir. assign opus where it matters and sonnet for the mechanical stuff. log every finding to its own file so we can promote the doc rewrites later
i need a structured deep dive of the whole monorepo with subagents working in parallel and a synthesis at the end. similar to that doc-sync run we did last quarter

the supabase pooler is timing out on every execute_sql call but the management API still works. i still want to start the audit now — can you run it against the migration files and mark anything we couldnt verify so we come back to it?
got 3 hours before standup. let's reconcile FRONTEND.md and INTEGRATION.md against what's actually shipped. parallelize as much as possible, give me a synthesis i can paste into slack
before our pre-IPO audit we need a full systems-level reality check on this app — schema, RLS, mutations, realtime, the whole thing. then update the architecture docs.
this one file `app/[locale]/(admin)/admin/files/page.tsx` is missing a permissiongate wrapper. can you add one with perm='fd_vap' and verify the layout already has getUser()?
our rule C6 in the guidelines says types should be named with underscores but everywhere in the code they use hyphens. just update the rule to match reality, no need for a big audit
we need docs for the new attendance upload feature. it goes through a CSV → ata edge function → atnd_punches insert. write me a markdown page explaining the flow.
we have a lot of tech debt items piling up in linear. help me categorize them by area (frontend, backend, infra) and rank by impact-vs-effort so we can plan a sprint
i want to refactor the transactions table — split it into transactions_header and transactions_lines. plan this out as a multi-phase migration with backfill, dual-write, cutover, cleanup.
create an ADR for choosing between trpc and rest for our new admin api. compare developer ergonomics, type safety, performance, and existing codebase fit
review the PR i just opened. it touches 4 files in apps/web/components — looking for security issues and consistency with the rest of the codebase
the audit log table got hit with 50M rows last month and queries are slow. partition it by month? add an index? lay out options.
explain how the file_class hierarchy works in this app. just walk me through the model — i don't need a deep audit, i just want to understand it
we got a security advisory from supabase saying 4 of our RLS policies have auth_rls_initplan warnings. fix them — they need (select auth.fn()) instead of auth.fn()
